source_intake_record.raw_question_text: As shown in the figure, in $\triangle ABC$, $\angle ACB=90^\circ$, $\angle B=30^\circ$, $CD$ is the height on the hypotenuse $AB$, $AD=3$, then what is the length of $BD$?
source_intake_record.raw_answer_text:
\textbf{Solution:} Since CD$\perp$AB and $\angle$ACB=$90^\circ$,\\
it follows that $\angle$ADC=$90^\circ=\angle$ACB,\\
since $\angle$B=$30^\circ$,\\
it follows that $\angle$A$=90^\circ-\angle$B$=60^\circ$,\\
thus $\angle$ACD$=90^\circ-\angle$A$=30^\circ$,\\
since AD$=3$,\\
it follows that AC$=2$AD$=6$,\\
thus AB$=2$AC$=12$,\\
hence BD$=AB-AD$=$12-3$=$\boxed{9}$

image_dependency: question_text explicitly says 'As shown in the figure'
asset_registry_record shows 1 image exists and registry_passed=true
However, the key geometric facts needed for solving (right angle at C, angle B=30°, CD altitude to AB, AD=3) are all stated in text, so the figure appears helpful but not strictly necessary
multi_step: Solving requires multiple linked deductions rather than direct lookup
The provided solution combines angle reasoning (derive angle A=60° and angle ACD=30°) with triangle properties
The solution then uses a 30-60-90 relation in triangle ACD to get AC from AD, then another relation in triangle ABC to get AB, then computes BD=AB-AD
This is a clear multi-step geometry chain involving at least two to three dependent inferences
verifiability: The target answer is a single numeric length (BD), which is easy to check
The provided answer ends with a concrete boxed value 9
The reasoning uses standard geometric relations that can be independently verified from the stated conditions
question_present=true, answer_present=true, and registry_passed=true support downstream checking
